title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After discussing a backpack with Mrs. Habberd, Poirot travels to a store on the main road to investigate its origin. Although Mrs. Habberd misremembered the name as Maberry or Kyles, Poirot identifies the shop as Hicks, which sells outdoor and sports equipment. Using an exotic accent, Poirot adopts the persona of a man buying a canvas backpack for a nephew going on a hiking trip. He purchases a sturdy canvas backpack identical to the one found in Colin's room. During the transaction, the shopkeeper mentions that many students from a nearby dormitory on Chestnut Street shop there.

Upon leaving, Poirot meets Inspector Sharpe, and the two meet at a sandwich shop to discuss the investigation. Sharpe reports that while a student named Chapman had three poisons in his possession, Sharpe doubts his guilt due to his lack of ill will toward Celia Austin. Sharpe also reveals that Elizabeth Johnston was a Communist, a discovery that surprises Poirot. Additionally, Sharpe notes that Patrica Lane had a handkerchief stained with green ink in her drawer, suggesting she may have been involved in an incident involving Elizabeth Johnston's papers. Sharpe also mentions that Leonard Bateson's father is a patient at Longveil Asylum and notes that Ahmed Ali possessed sexual books and postcards. Sharpe further describes finding subversive pamphlets among Chandra Lal's belongings and a large number of empty brandy bottles in Mrs. Nikolaitis's private cabinet. Despite these findings, the search for illegal passports yielded nothing. Poirot concludes that the investigation must return to the backpack as the starting point of the events. Meanwhile, Mrs. Nikolaitis is seen ascending the stairs after an argument with Jerome and Maria, where she makes disparaging remarks about Italians.
